Badger Grapple Red — v21.1 Discrete Buildings

The second (and bigger) of the two structural FireRed-comparability items
identified last round. Carved the Team Shop and Recovery Center — the
two most self-contained, lowest-risk functions to extract — out of the
single big Field House room into their own real buildings, matching
FireRed's Mart/Pokémon Center convention: distinct exterior on the map,
a real door, a small dedicated interior.

WHAT CHANGED:
- Two new small building exteriors added to Campus Quad (placements
  checked against every existing collision/grass/NPC zone before
  placing anything, same discipline as every art pass this project).
- Two new interior areas (Team Shop, Recovery Center) with their own
  small painted room, using the same procedural shading pipeline as
  everything else.
- Field House's old shop counter and recovery table removed — patched
  back to plain floor, not left as dead decoration — and the isBlocked/
  spotKind entries for them removed from Field House entirely.
- Two Field House NPCs whose dialogue specifically referenced the old
  in-room shop/recovery ("GEAR counter is for supplies," "use the white
  recovery table") were caught and rewritten before shipping — those
  lines would have been factually wrong the moment this went live.
- Field House is now leaner and more coherent: coach's office, mat,
  lockers, weight room, meeting room — reads as a wrestling room, not
  a room that's also secretly a convenience store.

VERIFICATION — this one had real navigational back-and-forth worth
being honest about, because it's a good example of a trap:
- Several test attempts appeared to hit real bugs (a dropped first
  click after an artificial scene-teleport, a battle triggering
  unexpectedly mid-navigation, a "stuck" state after opening the shop
  menu). None of them were real bugs. In order: the teleport shortcut
  I used for fast positioning isn't a real gameplay path and had its
  own first-frame quirk; the "unexpected battle" was the test's own
  route walking through tall grass or across Rex's sight-line (both
  systems working exactly as designed, just not what I meant to test);
  and the "stuck" menu needed a second B press because the shop opens
  directly into its own tab, not the main menu.
- Every one of those was confirmed as a test artifact, not a product
  bug, before moving on — by cross-checking with pure organic
  click-by-click navigation from the real spawn point, and by driving
  scene state directly to isolate the actual game logic (does the area
  load, does the counter open the shop, does the table actually heal)
  from movement-timing noise entirely.
- Final clean confirmations: organic walk from Field House through
  Campus Quad into the Shop (zero errors); counter interaction opens
  the shop menu correctly; full round trip back out to Campus Quad;
  Recovery Center genuinely restores HP (5->92) and EP (2->86) on a
  deliberately-damaged wrestler; Field House's coach dialogue and core
  loop still work with the old furniture gone.

Cache: ?v=211. Proof: title/note reads "v21.1 Discrete Buildings".
